description: Translation YAML with no locale wrapper → TranslationMissingLocaleKey error

errors.message: Translation file has no top-level locale key. Top-level keys found: app. Wrap the entire tree in the file's locale (e.g. `en:`) — platformOS indexes translations by locale at the root. Without this wrapper, `{{ 'key' | t }}` lookups will silently fail even though the file parses.
errors.hint:
Translation file is missing the top-level locale wrapper. platformOS indexes translations under `<locale>:` at the YAML root — without it `{{ '...' | t }}` lookups silently return the raw key, even though the file parses cleanly.

Wrap the entire tree under `en:` and indent every existing line by two spaces. Example transform:
```yaml
# BEFORE (broken — no locale root)
app:
  greeting: "Hello"
```
```yaml
# AFTER (correct)
en:
  app:
    greeting: "Hello"
```
One-liner with sed: `sed -E 's/^(.+)/  \1/' file.yml | (echo 'en:'; cat) > file.yml.tmp && mv file.yml.tmp file.yml` (verify after — re-run validate_code on the file).

see_also.reason: Translations domain guide explains the locale-root requirement and lookup conventions.

fixes.description: Add `en:` as the new top-level key in this file, then indent every existing line by two more spaces so the original tree nests under it. Re-run validate_code to confirm.

result.next_step:
Fix every ERROR above.
Re-validate with validate_code (mode: "quick") after fixing.
MUST NOT write the file to disk until validation passes (must_fix_before_write: false).
